steps.title: Recognize the mid-sentence appositive
steps.content: The phrase “a lawyer and cycling advocate” is an appositive—a noun phrase that renames “Ernesto Hernandez-Lopez” and appears between the subject and the main verb “has identified”.

steps.title: Apply the appositive punctuation rule
steps.content:
An appositive phrase positioned mid-sentence must be enclosed by commas on both sides. Choice B correctly applies this rule by placing commas before and after the phrase. Choices A, C, and D omit one or both required commas, violating the rule.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
